name: Poblet and Prades Mountains Loop
name_original: Montañas de Prades – circular desde Poblet por Prades y Montblanc
description: Cycle a difficult 42.0-mile (67.5 km) loop through the Montañas de Prades, visiting Poblet, Prades, and Montblanc with 3796 feet of climbing
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Vimbodí i Poblet are set within the mountainous landscapes of the Conca de Barberà region in Catalonia, characterized by the Prades mountains and the Catalan Pre-Coastal Range. The area features significant elevation gains, with routes traversing diverse terrain including paved surfaces, natural parks, and unique geological formations. Cyclists can explore the Poblet Natural Park, known for its holm oak reserves and the Francolí River, alongside striking rock formations like Els Frares Encantats. The region offers a network of routes suitable for various fitness levels, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Vimbodí i Poblet The most popular road cycling route is Poblet and Prades Mountains Loop, a 41.9 miles (67.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbing towards the Coll d'Arena Pass and passes through historic towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de la Mola (1010 m) – Main Square of Prades loop from l'Espluga de Francolí, a moderate 31.3 miles (50.4 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and leads to the historic town of Prades. Local road cyclists also love the Coll de Capafonts–Prades – Mont-ral to La Mussara Road loop from Prades, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail leading through the Prades mountains, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Vimbodí i Poblet is defined by mountainous terrain, the Prades mountains, and the Poblet Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in Vimbodí i Poblet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 3,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vimbodí i Poblet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Vimbodí i Poblet?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes around Vimbodí i Poblet, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse network of trails, from moderate rides to challenging ascents, all set within the region's mountainous landscapes.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes in Vimbodí i Poblet?
A: Road cycling routes in Vimbodí i Poblet feature significant elevation gains, often ranging from 500 feet to over 1,500 feet. Some challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962733193/poblet-and-prades-mountains-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblet and Prades Mountains Loop&lt;/a&gt;, include substantial climbs towards passes like Coll d'Arena, with total ascents exceeding 1,000 meters.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The terrain around Vimbodí i Poblet is predominantly mountainous, with routes traversing the Prades mountains and the Catalan Pre-Coastal Range. Most routes feature well-maintained paved surfaces, ideal for road cycling. You'll experience winding roads and notable gradients, especially on climbs.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, the region offers several challenging climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962733193/poblet-and-prades-mountains-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblet and Prades Mountains Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes a substantial 19-kilometer climb towards the Coll d'Arena Pass. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Alforja (641 m) – Coll d'Albarca loop from l'Espluga de Francolí&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1,700 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You'll encounter breathtaking natural features, including the &lt;strong&gt;Poblet Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its holm oak reserves and ancient chestnut trees. Keep an eye out for unique geological formations like &lt;strong&gt;Els Frares Encantats&lt;/strong&gt; ('The Enchanted Friars') in the Pena ravine, and the granites of the &lt;strong&gt;Castellfollit Valley&lt;/strong&gt;. The highest point, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/632147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tossal de la Baltasana summit&lt;/a&gt;, also offers impressive views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the cycling routes?
A: While directly on road cycling routes, you can find several beautiful waterfalls and natural pools nearby. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3575157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l'Olla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/482088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagle's Nest Natural Pool and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1109069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Mas dels Frares Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These are often short detours from main roads.

Q: What cultural landmarks can I visit while road cycling?
A: The region is rich in cultural landmarks. The &lt;strong&gt;Royal Monastery of Santa Maria de Poblet&lt;/strong&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is a popular starting point for many routes. You can also cycle through historic towns like Prades, known as the 'Red Place,' and past the medieval walls of Montblanc. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14915980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Baixa – Sant Jordi Wall, Montblanc loop from Vimbodí&lt;/a&gt; is one route that passes through Montblanc.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Vimbodí i Poblet?
A: The favorable climate makes spring and autumn ideal seasons for road cycling in Vimbodí i Poblet. During these times, the weather is generally pleasant, allowing for comfortable rides and enjoying the natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962733193/poblet-and-prades-mountains-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblet and Prades Mountains Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive tour of the region's highlights.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Vimbodí i Poblet?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning natural and cultural landmarks encountered along the routes.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While the region is known for its mountainous terrain, there are options for less experienced cyclists. Out of over 100 routes, 44 are classified as moderate and 6 as easy, offering less strenuous rides while still allowing you to enjoy the scenic landscapes.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near charming villages and towns like Prades and Capafonts, where you can find cafes and restaurants to refuel. These stops offer a chance to experience local cuisine and take a break during your ride.
tips: The Coll de Capafonts is located in the middle of the Muntanyes de Prades - one of the largest mountain ranges in the province of Tarragona.

This mountain road passes through the villages of La Riba, El Pinetell, and Farena, alongside the Brugent River. It's a peaceful route with beautiful views. In the shaded area, there is a risk of frost in winter. The road has good asphalt and two lanes from La Riba to Farena, with gradients between 8 and 12% and some flat sections and descents between El Pinetell and Farena. From Farena onwards, the road narrows to a single lane, but the asphalt remains good, with gradients exceeding 15%. The road joins the TV-7041 from Mont-ral to Capafonts at La Cadeneta.

Monastery founded in 1150, when the church, the monks' refectory, the Sant Esteve chapel, the infirmary and part of the cloister were built. During the 13th century, the rest of the buildings in the complex were completed, with everything the monks needed for their lives here: chapter house, bedroom, cloister, kitchen, the scriptorium, the hospital for the poor and pilgrims, and the chapel of Santa Caterina. In later centuries the dome and the walls, the pantheon and the other royal constructions were added.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness. It features significant elevation gains, with over 1,100 meters of climbing across its 67.5 km distance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take around 3 hours and 31 minutes to complete, assuming a demanding pace due to the elevation profile.

faqs.question: Where does the Montañas de Prades circular route start?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts near the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Poblet, a UNESCO World Heritage site. This provides an excellent cultural starting point.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the starting point?
faqs.answer: Yes, parking is generally available near the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Poblet, which serves as the starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect outstandingly beautiful and breathtaking scenery throughout the ride. The route passes through ancient forests, offers panoramic viewpoints, and showcases the rocky outcrops of the Prades Mountains. You'll also cycle through tiny, charming villages and see the distinctive red-brick architecture of Prades.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Royal Monastery of Santa María de Poblet at the start, the medieval town of Montblanc with its well-preserved walled enclosure, and Prades, known as the 'Red Village' for its unique architecture and charming town square. You'll also traverse mountain passes like Coll de Capafonts and La Riba–Farena Mountain Road.

faqs.question: What is the road surface like on this route?
faqs.answer: The majority of the route is on well-paved, narrow, and twisty asphalt roads, which are highly enjoyable for road cyclists. These roads are known for having minimal traffic, allowing for an immersive cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montañas de Prades region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the mountain air offers some relief. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Montañas de Prades?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Montañas de Prades. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas or private land you might pass near.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its significant elevation gain and length. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children looking for a leisurely ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths, including sections of the CIMA TA03 Prades (Coll de l'Arena), El Brogit de la Vall, and the Ruta del Císter.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Montblanc and Prades offer excellent opportunities for cafe stops and restaurants. Montblanc's historic center and Prades' charming town square are ideal spots to refuel and enjoy local cuisine.

waypoints.name: Royal Monastery of Santa María de Poblet
